skill.name: specdd-adopt
skill.description: Use this skill to add or update SpecDD framework files in a repository through the SpecDD CLI before normal SpecDD work begins.
skill.summary: Adopt SpecDD in a repository through a controlled CLI setup workflow.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and flagged wording as weak cryptography or reconnaissance. Review found no scripts, network access, secret access, or prompt injection. The skill directs operator-approved SpecDD CLI use, so it has low operational risk.

low_findings.title: Operator-Approved CLI and File Modification
low_findings.description: The skill directs use of the SpecDD CLI to initialize or update framework files. It limits this action to operator-requested setup or updates.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly instructs CLI use and controlled file creation. Risk is limited because execution is requested by the operator and scoped to SpecDD files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported repeated Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations are Markdown inline-code references, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected file is Markdown documentation. Backticks wrap file paths, skill names, and CLI names rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flag Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The flagged description line contains SpecDD setup wording. No weak hash, cipher, cryptographic function, or credential handling appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain frontmatter text describing the skill. I found no cryptographic API or algorithm reference in the file.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Flag Is Scoped Project Inspection
low_findings.description: The flagged area routes work to the correct SpecDD skill and limits inspection to repository shape. It does not request host or secret enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text limits inspection to project structure needed for adoption boundaries. No evidence found of system, network, or credential reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Teams need a controlled way to introduce SpecDD without guessing project boundaries. This skill guides CLI setup, bootstrap checks, and initial spec scoping.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a repository already has SpecDD bootstrap files or contracts.
Uses the SpecDD CLI for operator-requested setup or framework updates.
Reads the active bootstrap chain before proposing local specifications.
Scopes initial specs around project boundaries, references, and ownership.
Reports setup status, adoption scope, and remaining user tasks.
limitations: Requires the SpecDD CLI or a related CLI skill to be available.
Does not author detailed specs when another task-specific SpecDD skill is more appropriate.
Does not modify implementation files unless the user also requests implementation work.
Creates specification files only after explicit user request or approval.

use_cases.title: Start SpecDD in an Existing Codebase
use_cases.description: Check the repository state, initialize bootstrap files, and propose a small first specification scope.

use_cases.title: Refresh SpecDD Framework Files
use_cases.description: Update existing SpecDD bootstrap files through the CLI when the operator asks for framework maintenance.

use_cases.title: Plan Initial Ownership Boundaries
use_cases.description: Inspect repository shape and outline path-based ownership before detailed specification authoring begins.

prompt_templates.title: Check Adoption Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-adopt to check whether this repository already has SpecDD bootstrap files or contracts. Report the setup state only.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize SpecDD
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-adopt to add SpecDD to this repository through the SpecDD CLI. Show the files created and any setup left to me.

prompt_templates.title: Update Framework Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-adopt to update existing SpecDD framework files through the CLI. Do not change implementation files.

prompt_templates.title: Propose Adoption Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-adopt to inspect the repository shape and propose the smallest useful initial SpecDD scope with ownership boundaries.

output_examples.input: Check whether this project is ready for SpecDD adoption.
output_examples.output: Repository is not bootstrapped. No active SpecDD contracts were found. Recommended next step is CLI initialization, then a root project spec.

output_examples.input: Update our SpecDD framework files, but do not touch application code.
output_examples.output: Framework update completed through the CLI. Application files were not changed. Review the bootstrap files before creating new local specs.

output_examples.input: Propose the first useful SpecDD scope for this repository.
output: Start with a root project spec that defines repository-wide behavior and authority.
Add child specs only where ownership boundaries are clear.
Label assumptions until maintainers confirm them.

best_practices: Run the CLI workflow only when the user asks for setup or framework updates.
Read the bootstrap chain before proposing or creating local specifications.
Keep initial specifications short, scoped, and tied to path-based ownership.
anti_patterns: Do not manually copy or edit SpecDD framework bootstrap files outside the CLI.
Do not create specification files without explicit user approval.
Do not present guessed behavior as an active project contract.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps a repository adopt SpecDD through a CLI-based setup or framework update workflow.

faq.question: Does it modify source code?
faq.answer: No. It avoids implementation changes unless the user explicitly asks for implementation work too.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before normal SpecDD work when a repository needs bootstrap setup or framework updates.

faq.question: Can it create specification files?
faq.answer: Yes, but only when the user explicitly requests or approves the adoption scope.

faq.question: Does it require a CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. If the SpecDD CLI is unavailable, setup or update work is blocked.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but users should review CLI-driven file changes.
